steps.title: Set up the equation for doubling
steps.content:
The initial population at $t = 0$ is $f(0) = 60,000(2)^{0} = 60,000$. When the population doubles, it equals $2 \times 60,000 = 120,000$. Set up the equation:

$$60,000(2)^{\frac{t}{580}} = 120,000$$

steps.title: Solve using exponent properties
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 60,000:

$$(2)^{\frac{t}{580}} = 2$$

Rewrite the right side as a power of 2: $(2)^{\frac{t}{580}} = 2^1$. Since the bases are equal, the exponents must be equal:

$$\frac{t}{580} = 1$$

Multiply both sides by 580 to get $t = 580$ minutes.
